plan_binding_snapshot.title: CP00-296 RP08.P08.M06.S09-RP08.P09.M03.S22 generated_from_weighted_implementation_ledger
plan_binding_snapshot.renumbering_policy: Pack IDs are monotonic after the latest live closeout pack. If a requested locked queue skips live-open ledger units, live-open units are inserted first and requested packs are shifted with correction_reason.

implementation_summary.summary: CP00-296 implements descriptor-only coverage that closes the RP08.P08 Hermes phase (M06 fixture tail, M07-M08 full Hermes cycles, M09 Claude review packet, M10 closeout head) and opens the RP08.P09 review foundation (M00-M03: architecture/security/permission-bypass/audit-completeness/missing-test/UI-leak/downstream-readiness questions, risk registers, severity taxonomies, go/no-go verdict formats, finding routing maps, human approval summaries, Claude review packets, closeout criteria, PASS/PASS_WITH_FINDINGS/BLOCK closeout notes, next RP dependencies, review receipt placeholders, future correction slots) across nine sections over 150 planned rows with Hermes evidence (H08), Claude review packet (C08), and CP00-297 handoff, without opening Hermes runtime receipts, command runtime execution, state writes, object storage, Citation Ledger, or Loop behavior.

parsed_review.summary: CP00-296 is a descriptor-only pack closing the RP08.P08 Hermes phase (M06 fixture tail, M07-M08 Hermes cycles, M09 Claude review packet, M10 closeout head) and opening the RP08.P09 review foundation (M00-M03), as nine descriptor-only sections over 150 planned units (RP08.P08.M06.S09-RP08.P09.M03.S22, risk C). Independent read-only inspection (Read/Grep/Glob) confirms the pack-level claim is correctly scoped and NOT overclaimed: command-evidence.json and manifest.json both report production_ready=false / implemented_pending_review, and the production_ready_flag is a descriptor-verification label rather than an enterprise-trust claim. Unit count and the deliverable (15 claude_review / 42 hermes_evidence / 73 implementation / 8 security_audit / 8 test / 4 ui), phase (P08=88, P09=62), micro-phase, and micro-title distributions reconcile across registry.js, service.js, validators.js, the contract, the plan, the queue, and the manifest, and are deterministically enforced by validateEmailDmsCoreCp296Coverage and the contract validator (assertions at validate-rp08-email-dms-core-contract.mjs:1195-1221) plus three dedicated tests (model.test.js:1568-1619). No-write/no-real-data/no-runtime boundaries are strongly attested (freezeCp296Result service.js:3385; CP296_NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION registry.js:2266; manifest.json:5905-5940) and the package contains zero side-effecting patterns (require/exec/fs.write/fetch/http/process.env/eval). The authority boundary holds at every enforcement point: Claude is read-only and explicitly not the final approver, human final approval is required for any runtime opening, and enterprise trust is not claimed from local validation. CP178+ review hardening is present (forbidden_review_evidence covers auth failures, zero-byte stdout, malformed/fenced JSON, tool-call-shaped output, missing fields, and final-approval/enterprise-trust claims), and the readiness auth-preflight passed with valid non-empty JSON. Hermes control-plane patterns are confined to deterministic review packet/receipt descriptors (emits_hermes_runtime_receipt=false; hermes_runtime_embedded_in_product=false). No P0/P1/P2 issues found; one non-blocking P3 documentation nit in the changed-file-scope classification. Final approval and enterprise trust remain a human responsibility outside this read-only review.

findings.title: changed-file-scope.json dual-classifies pack-related untracked paths under 'unrelated_dirty_files_preserved'
findings.details: 'docs/closeout-pack-plan/new-session-handoff.md' and 'docs/closeout-packs/cp00-296/' appear in both untracked_implementation_files (correctly identifying them as part of this pack) and unrelated_dirty_files_preserved. The latter field's name implies the paths are not part of the pack, yet docs/closeout-packs/cp00-296/ holds this pack's own manifest.json and command-evidence.json. The genuinely unrelated dirty files (the v2.0 .docx and docs/ldip-integration/.../c-ldip-01-invalid-attempt-01.json) are correctly excluded, and the authoritative pack scope (active_pack_implementation_refs / tracked-modified files) is unambiguous, so this has no effect on the review outcome or on pack scoping.
findings.recommendation: Clarify the semantics of unrelated_dirty_files_preserved (e.g. rename to untracked_or_preserved_dirty_files, or exclude paths already listed in untracked_implementation_files) so a strict reader is not misled into treating the pack's own docs directory as unrelated.
findings.post_review_disposition: Informational only; non-blocking. Does not affect pack or goal closeout. Recommend cosmetic cleanup in the scope-generation tooling at a future convenience.

commit_policy.message: Close CP00-296 email dms core p08 closeout p09 review foundation descriptor pack
